original_body:
Expose warm_start in Isolation forest
It seems to me that `sklearn.ensemble.IsolationForest` supports incremental addition of new trees with the `warm_start` parameter of its parent class, `sklearn.ensemble.BaseBagging`.

Even though this parameter is not exposed in `__init__()` , it gets inherited from `BaseBagging` and one can use it by changing it to `True` after initialization. To make it work, you have to also increment `n_estimators` on every iteration. 

It took me a while to notice that it actually works, and I had to inspect the source code of both `IsolationForest` and `BaseBagging`. Also, it looks to me that the behavior is in-line with `sklearn.ensemble.BaseForest` that is behind e.g. `sklearn.ensemble.RandomForestClassifier`.

To make it more easier to use, I'd suggest to:
* expose `warm_start` in `IsolationForest.__init__()`, default `False`;
* document it in the same way as it is documented for `RandomForestClassifier`, i.e. say:
```py
    warm_start : bool, optional (default=False)
        When set to ``True``, reuse the solution of the previous call to fit
        and add more estimators to the ensemble, otherwise, just fit a whole
        new forest. See :term:`the Glossary <warm_start>`.
```
* add a test to make sure it works properly;
* possibly also mention in the "IsolationForest example" documentation entry;
enhanced_title: Add warm_start parameter to IsolationForest for incremental training
enhanced_body:
## Summary
The IsolationForest class inherits warm_start functionality from its parent class BaseBagging, but this parameter is not exposed in its __init__() method. This makes it difficult for users to discover and use incremental training capabilities.

## Current Behavior
- IsolationForest inherits warm_start from BaseBagging but doesn't expose it in its interface
- Users must manually set warm_start=True after initialization to use incremental training
- Users must also manually increment n_estimators on each iteration
- This behavior is inconsistent with other forest estimators like RandomForestClassifier which properly expose warm_start

## Expected Behavior
- warm_start parameter should be exposed in IsolationForest.__init__() with default False
- Parameter should be documented consistently with other forest estimators
- Should support incremental training by reusing previous solution when warm_start=True

## Reproduction Steps
1. Initialize an IsolationForest instance
2. Try to set warm_start=True directly in __init__() - this will fail
3. Current workaround:
   ```python
   iso = IsolationForest(n_estimators=10)
   iso.warm_start = True  # Must set after initialization
   iso.n_estimators = 20  # Must increment manually
   iso.fit(X_train)
   iso.n_estimators = 30  # Must increment again
   iso.fit(X_additional)  # Continues training
   ```

## Affected Components
- sklearn/ensemble/_iforest.py - Main IsolationForest implementation
- Documentation for IsolationForest class
- Example documentation for IsolationForest

## Environment
- scikit-learn version: Any (issue affects all versions)
- Python version: Any
- This is a code organization/documentation issue, not version-specific

## Proposed Solution
1. Add warm_start parameter to IsolationForest.__init__() with default False
2. Add documentation matching RandomForestClassifier:
   ```python
   warm_start : bool, optional (default=False)
       When set to True, reuse the solution of the previous call to fit
       and add more estimators to the ensemble, otherwise, just fit a whole
       new forest. See :term:`the Glossary <warm_start>`.
   ```
3. Add test cases to verify incremental training works correctly
4. Update IsolationForest example documentation to mention warm_start capability

## Benefits
- Consistent API with other forest estimators
- Better discoverability of incremental training feature
- Easier to use for users who want to add trees incrementally
- Maintains backward compatibility (default remains False)
